title: The Science of Memory
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The (0) complexity of human memory continues to fascinate scientists and psychologists alike. Memory is not a single process but involves multiple (17) interconnected systems in the brain. Research has shown that (18) emotional experiences are often remembered more vividly than neutral ones, which explains why (19) significant events from childhood stay with us throughout our lives.||Scientists have made (20) considerable progress in understanding how memories are stored and retrieved. However, memory is also (21) surprisingly unreliable; it can be distorted by (22) suggestion and other outside influences. Developing effective (23) strategies for improving memory has become an important area of study, with (24) practical applications in education, healthcare, and everyday life.

example.text: The (0) COMPLEXITY of human memory continues to fascinate scientists and psychologists alike. (COMPLEX)

questions.explanation: The adjective 'interconnected' uses the prefix 'inter-' and the suffix '-ed' added to 'connect'.
The adjective 'emotional' is formed from 'emotion' by adding the suffix '-al'.
The adjective 'significant' is formed from 'signify' by replacing '-ify' with '-icant'.
The adjective 'considerable' is formed from 'consider' by adding the suffix '-able'.
The adverb 'surprisingly' is formed from 'surprise' by adding '-ingly'.
The noun 'suggestion' is formed from 'suggest' by adding the suffix '-ion'.
The plural noun 'strategies' is formed from 'strategic' via 'strategy' + '-ies'.
The adjective 'practical' is formed from 'practice' by replacing '-ice' with '-ical'.
